_meta.name: CLM r4 hexa-native CUDA error 700 — re-occurrence on fresh pod

training_run.context: Smoke run with STEPS=20 — production target is 750K. Smoke failed at step 3.

init_phase_succeeded: cuda_ffi loaded (CUDA + cuBLAS bindings, 23 batch GEMM)
20 kernels (12 base + 8 fused) NVRTC compiled OK from cache .hexa-cache/ptx_162369251.ptx

failure.fatal_message: [train_clm_kr] FATAL — sample_batch returned null at step=3 micro=6
failure.error_700_meaning: CUDA_ERROR_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS — kernel launched with bad pointer or array oob

root_cause_hypotheses.claim: An earlier kernel writes through a freed/reused pointer; subsequent cuda_malloc returns 700 because the context is wedged
root_cause_hypotheses.evidence: First failure was cuLaunchKernelEx (shmem=0), then cascade of cuda_malloc failures. Once a CUDA context hits illegal-address, all subsequent allocations on it fail.
root_cause_hypotheses.fix_direction: Add cudaDeviceSynchronize + cudaGetLastError after every kernel launch in gpu_train hot loop; identify which kernel poisons the context.

root_cause_hypotheses.claim: Some kernel indexes [SEQ] when it should index [SEQ-1] or accesses position SEQ in a SEQ-size buffer
root_cause_hypotheses.evidence: 12 base + 8 fused kernels; bytes-only vocab 256 should mean small offsets; SEQ=512 hardcoded
root_cause_hypotheses.fix_direction: Run with cuda-memcheck (compute-sanitizer) to localize the OOB access kernel

root_cause_hypotheses.claim: Per memory project_session_20260412_night.md and feedback_clm_hexa_gpu_plan.md, prior CLM hexa GPU runs hit cuLaunchKernelEx failures on H100 SM90 builds — same family of bug not fully resolved
root_cause_hypotheses.evidence: Same error fingerprint as h100_clm_native_debug.md / h100_clm_native_dmon.log
root_cause_hypotheses.fix_direction: Compare working pod build vs this pod's hxblas/hxccl/hxflash/hxlayer/hxlmhead/hxqwen14b/hxvdsp .so versions; verify SM90 PTX targets in NVRTC compile flags

next_steps_for_repair_session: 1. Re-launch with COMPUTE_SANITIZER=1 (or compute-sanitizer wrapper) to localize the kernel that triggers 700
2. Dump the .ptx-cached kernel that NVRTC compiled (.hexa-cache/ptx_162369251.ptx) and disassemble first
3. Compare anima-engines/cuda_kernels.hexa kernel definitions vs the 20 entry point list — confirm shape mismatches between kernel signatures and runtime call args
4. Add per-kernel cudaPeekAtLastError() after each launch in gpu_train.hexa hot loop
blocked_track: CLM r4 750K production launch is BLOCKED until smoke (STEPS=20) passes. Cannot proceed to 41h run with confidence the binary will stay alive.

live_state_summary.ALM_track: FROZEN (Python ban + serve down + no hexa-native LoRA trainer)
live_state_summary.CLM_track: BLOCKED (hexa-native trainer crashes at step 3 on H100 SM90)
live_state_summary.AGI_dual_track_progress: STALLED — both arms gated on hexa-lang infrastructure work
